baseline.note: Current estimation after SC-001. Baseline section removed from deliverable — see CLAUDE.md for context.

items.clientAnswer: Yes — authentication should be handled via Microsoft Entra (Azure AD), and application roles can be mapped from Entra roles or group membership. Application role enforcement must still exist at the application layer, not only at the identity layer.
items.description: Confirmed Azure AD Groups as auth approach (Option A). App-layer enforcement already included in estimation.
estimationItems: #5 Azure AD Group setup (15 hrs) — already included
#6 Dynamic role-based authorization (21 hrs) — already included

items.clientAnswer: No CSV sample yet, but provided mock InventoryItem schema with fields: ID, InventorySource, PartNumber, Description, WarehouseLocation, Condition, QtyTotal, QtyCommitted, QtyAvailable, Uom, Status, SaleTypeTag, MetadataJson. Constraints: QtyCommitted ≤ QtyTotal, both ≥ 0.
items.description: Confirmed inventory schema fields. No CSV template yet but schema clarity reduces Sprint 0 risk. Parsing + validation already covered in estimation.
estimationItems: #11 Bulk CSV/Excel upload (48 hrs) — already included

items.clientAnswer: Each inventory item is identified by a composite key: PartNumber + Condition + Location + Grade. Master description is maintained separately — net-new items populate master automatically; existing items retain source description only; master changes require Admin/Manager action.
items.description: Added inventory identity logic — composite uniqueness enforcement + master vs source description management

items.clientAnswer: Inventory uploads are CSV files with standard columns: PartNumber, Description, Quantity, Condition, Location, Grade, Cost. Uploads always create new adjustment records — never overwrite existing inventory.
items.description: Confirmed CSV upload format and behavior already modeled in estimation. Parsing + validation covered in item #11.
estimationItems: #11 Bulk CSV/Excel upload (48 hrs) — already included

items.clientAnswer: One active bid per customer per line item per offer. When a bid is updated, the system creates a new revision (rev 2, rev 3, etc.) — previous revisions are retained for audit but only the latest is active.
items.description: Added bid uniqueness enforcement + revision tracking (one active bid per customer/line/offer, previous revisions retained for audit)
estimationItems: #20 Bid uniqueness enforcement + revision tracking (15 hrs)

items.clientAnswer: Validation rules: Quantity ≤ available inventory (unless partial allocation later), Quantity > 0, Price > 0, Offer must be active (within date range), Customer context required, User role enforcement.
items.description: Confirmed standard validation rules already modeled in estimation. No additional validation beyond what was assumed.
estimationItems: #19 MudBlazor DataGrid — in-cell editing (56 hrs) — validation included
#21 Bid types support (18 hrs) — validation included

items.clientAnswer: When two or more bids tie on price/quantity, the system surfaces the tie to the Sales Manager. Manager resolves manually by selecting the winning bid — no automatic tie-breaking logic.
items.description: Added tie detection logic + resolution UI (system surfaces tied bids, manager picks winner manually)
estimationItems: #31 Tie detection logic + resolution UI (12 hrs)

items.clientAnswer: Full reversal must be supported in Phase 1. When an allocation is reversed: inventory returns to Available status, the allocation is marked as Reversed (with reason code), and the export is locked/invalidated.
items.description: Added full allocation reversal support — return inventory to Available, mark Reversed, lock/regenerate export, require reason code with audit trail
estimationItems: #30 Reversal reason code + audit trail (12 hrs)

items.clientAnswer: All allocations are manager-approved in Phase 1. No automatic approval thresholds. However, the system should show a visual 'below floor' warning when pricing is below a configurable floor — visual only, no auto-reject.
items.description: Simplified approval from configurable thresholds (21 hrs) to visual-only 'below floor' indicator (12 hrs). Thresholds moved to future phase.
estimationItems: #27 changed: Approval thresholds (21 hrs) → Visual pricing indicators (12 hrs)

items.trigger: A10 — Approval Authority: Manager vs Admin
items.clientAnswer: Sales Manager: within defined authority. Admin: full authority including override. Do not limit approval to manager only — admin must have escalation power.
items.description: Confirmed both Sales Manager and Admin can approve allocations. Admin has full override authority. Already covered in role-based access design.
estimationItems: #26 Approval workflow (48 hrs) — already includes manager + admin paths
#6 Dynamic role-based authorization (21 hrs) — already included

items.clientAnswer: N/A — automatic recalculation based on new MVP total
items.description: Bug buffer recalculated from 10% of pre-buffer MVP. Was 75 hrs (10% of ~750), now 90 hrs (10% of ~900).

summary.note: Client Q&A answers A1-A10: 5 confirmed (no impact), 4 added (+140 hrs new features), 1 modified (-9 hrs thresholds simplified), bug buffer recalculated (+15 hrs). Net impact: +146 hrs to MVP.
